mnemonics.title: DS Goals
mnemonics.content: SORCE: Sharing, Openness, Reliability, Concurrency, Efficiency (or Scalability)

mnemonics.title: Transparency Types
mnemonics.content: ALARM: Access, Location, Animation (Migration), Replication, Concurrency, Failure

mnemonics.title: CAP Theorem
mnemonics.content: During Partition, choose between Consistency or Availability - you can't have both when network fails

acronyms.usage: Collection of autonomous computers acting as one coherent system
Technique for client-server communication making remote calls appear local
Java's implementation of RPC for object-oriented distributed computing
Middleware standard enabling heterogeneous objects to communicate
Distributed naming service providing location transparency for internet resources
Decentralized architecture where each node can be client and server

visualTips.visualization: Draw a central server with multiple client nodes around it, showing request-response arrows. Add a cloud representing the network between them.
Draw nodes in a mesh or circle, each connected to several others with bidirectional arrows showing equal capability.
Create a layered pyramid: Bottom=Hardware/Network, Middle=Middleware, Top=Applications. Show arrows representing transparency hiding lower layers.
Use a triangle with C, A, P at corners. Show that P (partition) is mandatory in distributed systems, so you can only choose one of C or A.

analogies.analogy: A distributed system is like a group project where each team member works independently and communicates via messages, versus a single person doing all work alone.
It's like each person in different time zones has their own watch showing different times - there's no master clock everyone agrees on.
Like a power outage in one building of a city - some areas work while others don't, and it's hard for working areas to know exactly what's failed.

analogies.visualization: Analogy: A hotel concierge acts as middleware between guests (clients) and hotel services (servers), handling the complexity and heterogeneity behind the scenes.
